channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [General] Everything You Need to Know About Hero Conf UK 2026
item.description: Our must-attend event for PPC and Paid Social professionals returns to Brighton on Thursday 30th and Friday 1st May 2026. This will be our second year of bringing Hero Conf back to the UK audience and it is going to be even bigger and bolder than the last! Here’s some of what you

item.title: [Google Ads] Get More Leads with Google Ads [2026 Step by Step Tutorial]
item.description:
In 2026, getting leads on Google is easy... but getting profitable leads? That's a different story.
Most service businesses are paying a laziness tax, letting Google's AI chase cheap, junk clicks that never convert. In this tutorial, I'm breaking down exactly how to take back control of your Google

item.title: [General] Boost E-commerce SEO: Unlock Driving Traffic Now! #shorts
item.description: Turn search queries into compelling content pages. Learn how to create ultimate guides and informative pages that target keywords like 'most forgiving driver' to drive SEO traffic to your e-commerce site. #EcommerceSEO #ContentMarketing #KeywordResearch #DigitalStrategy #SEOTips

item.title: [Meta Ads] What Meta Isn't Telling You About Your Creative
item.description: Meta provides no transparency about which specific images or videos perform best when using flexible format, related media, or AI generated creative. Breakdowns exist but share almost no useful detail. Jon explains why this lack of transparency is intentional at this point, why it matters for creati

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Keyword Planner: AI for Local Service Keywords #shorts
item.description: Leverage AI tools like Claude or ChatGPT to extract valuable Google Ads keywords from Google Keyword Planner. Perfect for local service companies, e-commerce, and SaaS. #GoogleAds #KeywordPlanner #AItools #DigitalMarketing #LocalSEO

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta広告で結局どの画像サイズを用意すればいい？押さえるべきアスペクト比の考え方
item.description: Meta広告のクリエイティブを準備するとき、「結局どのサイズを用意すればいいの？」という疑問にぶつかる方は多いのではないでしょうか。配信面ごとに推奨サイズが異なるうえ、公式ヘルプの情報も分散していて全…
item.category: アナグラム ブログ

item.title: [General] Audience Segments Change Everything
[General] Google Business Profile Optimization White Label Solutions: Services, Pricing, SOPs, and KPIs for Agencies
item.description: Local search has matured into one of the most commercially important channels in digital marketing. When someone searches for a lawyer, dentist, med spa, HVAC company, accountant, roofer, or restaurant in a specific area, Google often routes that intent through map results and Business Profiles b

item.title: [General] White Label Citation Building: Done-for-You Directory Submissions for Better NAP Consistency and Local Search Visibility
item.description: If you run an agency, manage local SEO for clients, or support multi-location brands, citation work tends to create the same operational problem: everyone agrees it matters, but almost nobody wants to spend internal team hours doing it manually. That is why white label citation building exists. A

item.title: [General] White Label Shopify SEO: 21 Product Page Optimization Tactics That Improve Traffic and Conversions
item.description: Shopify product pages sit at the point where search visibility, merchandising, user experience, and revenue all meet. For agencies offering white label Shopify SEO, that makes the product page the most important place to get right. A category page may bring in broader discovery traffic. A blog ar

item.title: [Google Ads] E-commerce PPC Management: The Complete Guide to White Label Google Shopping Campaigns for Agencies and Online Stores
item.description: Google Shopping is one of the most commercially direct channels in e-commerce PPC. A user searches for a product, sees an image, price, merchant name, reviews or promotions, and clicks with a clearer idea of what they are about to buy. That is why Shopping traffic often behaves differently from s

item.title: [General] Core Web Vitals Optimization for Agencies: White Label Site Speed Fixes That Improve LCP, INP, CLS, SEO, and Conversions
item.description: If you run an agency, site speed is rarely just a technical issue. It becomes an account management issue, a retention issue, a margin issue, and eventually a growth issue. Clients do not usually call and say, “Our Interaction to Next Paint is too high.” They say their traffic has stalled, leads

item.title: [General] White Label Link Building Services: What Agencies Should Buy, What to Avoid, and How Quality Links Move Rankings
item.description: Link building is still one of the hardest SEO services for agencies to deliver well at scale. It is slow, relationship-driven, quality-sensitive, and easy to get wrong. Most agencies can sell SEO. Far fewer can consistently earn relevant, editorial backlinks that improve search visibility without

item.title: [General] Schema Markup Implementation: The Complete Guide to White Label Structured Data Services for Agencies
item.description: Search engines do not read websites the way people do. A human visitor can scan a page, recognize that it is a service page, notice the business name, identify the offer, and understand whether the company serves a local market or a national one. Search engines need more explicit signals. That is

item.title: [General] Amazon Marketing Services for Sellers: How White Label PPC + SEO Improve Visibility, Efficiency, and Sales
item.description: If you work with Amazon sellers, you have probably noticed that the phrase Amazon Marketing Services still shows up everywhere, even though Amazon’s ad ecosystem has evolved well beyond the original label. Sellers, agencies, and brand teams still use the term as shorthand for the work that actual

item.title: [General] Outreach Link Building for Agencies: The White Label Solutions Teams Are Switching To for Better Links, Safer Delivery, and Stronger Client Retention
item.description: When agencies talk about link building, they often use one phrase to describe several very different services. One vendor sells placements from a fixed website inventory. Another pitches digital PR. Another offers niche edits. Another runs outreach but outsources content. Another buys media place

item.title: [General] How to Resell Local SEO: The Complete Guide to Pricing, Fulfillment, and Client Retention for Marketing Agencies
item.description: If you run a marketing agency, sooner or later a client asks a version of the same question: can you help us show up higher in Google Maps, local search, and “near me” results? For many agencies, that moment creates a choice. You can say no and leave revenue on the table. You can try […]

item.title: [General] Orkin SEO Strategy: Keyword Research for Pest Control #shorts
item.description: Learn how to strategically pull keywords for a service-based business like Orkin. We explore targeting options for pest control, termite control, and more within home services. #KeywordResearch #PestControl #DigitalMarketing #SEOtips #Orkin

item.title: [Meta Ads] Audience Segments Change Everything
item.description: When you use audience segments, they will change your understanding of algorithmic targeting and remarketing. Learn how to create them, break down results, and apply what you learn in my free mini-course. Subscribe at jonloomer.com/segments-subscribe.

item.title: [Google Ads] New Google Ad Manager API read-only scope
item.description:
The Ad Manager API now supports a read-only OAuth scope. This new scope, https://www.googleapis.com/auth/admanager.readonly, allows developers to request read-only access to their users' Ad Manager data.

This new scope is only available for the

item.title: [Google Ads] Increase in Batch Job Service Request Size
item.description:
Increased Request Limits for BatchJobService Enhance Large Asset Uploads

We're pleased to announce significant increases to the request size limits for the BatchJobService.AddBatchJobOperations method in the Google Ads API. These changes are designed to str

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Debunking the myth: Is AI-powered search killing ad success?
item.description: AI isn’t replacing search - it’s reshaping it. Learn how Search First brands stay visible and influential across AI driven, zero click customer journeys.

item.title: [Google Ads] Master Google Ads: Keyword Planner Secrets Revealed! #shorts
item.description: Discover how to leverage Google Ads Keyword Planner for long-tail variations. Filter by monthly searches and top-of-page bids to find your most valuable keywords for SEO success. #KeywordPlanner #GoogleAds #SEOStrategy #DigitalMarketing

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Performance Max Seasonal Creative Theming: What Asset Group Theming Does, How It Works, and What Advertisers Should Do Next
item.description: Google has added seasonal creative theming to Performance Max asset groups, giving advertisers a faster way to adapt proven creative for holidays, promotions, and recurring seasonal moments. The update matters because it changes a part of Performance Max workflow that has traditionally been slow:

item.title: [General] ChatGPT Ads Explained: Where Ads Appear, Who Sees Them, How Targeting Works, and What Marketers Need to Do
item.description: For a long time, the idea of ads inside ChatGPT felt speculative. It was discussed, debated, and denied often enough that many marketers treated it like a future problem. That is no longer the right way to look at it. Ads are appearing in ChatGPT, they are appearing in real user sessions, and the

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Data Manager Adds ActiveCampaign Integration: What It Means for Customer Match, Offline Conversions, and Lead Quality
item.description: Google Ads has added ActiveCampaign as a supported data source inside Data Manager, a change that matters far beyond a simple connector update. For advertisers, agencies, CRM teams, and revenue operations leaders, this addition closes a meaningful gap between customer data stored in ActiveCampaig

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Advisor: What the AI Agent Does, How the Email Rollout Works, and What Advertisers Should Do Next
item.description: Google Ads Advisor is moving from “interesting new feature” territory into something more operationally important. The clearest signal is that Google is no longer limiting exposure to in-product placements and quiet interface tests. Advertisers are now seeing direct email promotion for Ads Adviso

item.title: [General] Google March 2026 Core Update: Confirmed Timeline, SEO Impact, and What Site Owners Should Do Next
item.description: Google has started rolling out the March 2026 core update, and for site owners, publishers, ecommerce brands, SaaS teams, and agencies, the question is not simply whether rankings will move. The more important question is what kind of response is actually useful while the rollout is still in prog

item.title: [General] Why Your Google Business Profile Map Pin Can Affect Local Rankings More Than Your Business Address
item.description: A business can do many things right and still underperform in local search. The Google Business Profile may be verified. The address may look correct. Categories may be accurate. Reviews may be coming in. The website may be optimized. And yet, visibility in Google Maps or the local pack can still

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads API v23.2 Explained: VideoEnhancement, AppTopCombinationView, New Metrics, and What Developers Need to Upgrade
item.description: Google has released v23.2 of the Google Ads API, and while this is a minor version update rather than a major platform reset, it includes several changes that matter for developers, agencies, in-house paid media teams, reporting engineers, and ad-tech platforms. The headlines are easy to spot: a

item.title: [General] ChatGPT Ads Reach $100 Million Annualized Revenue as OpenAI Opens Self-Serve Access in April
item.description: OpenAI’s advertising business inside ChatGPT has crossed a milestone faster than most new ad products do. Within roughly six weeks of launching its U.S. pilot, the company says ChatGPT ads have passed $100 million in annualized revenue, with self-serve advertiser access expected to open in April.

item.title: [General] What Drives AI Recommendations? The Real Role of Reddit, Wikipedia, and Topical Authority in AI Search
item.description: If a brand team looks only at headline charts about AI citations, the obvious conclusion seems simple: Reddit and Wikipedia are everywhere, so visibility in AI must come from winning those two platforms. That conclusion is incomplete. Reddit and Wikipedia do matter. They appear often in AI-genera

item.title: [General] YouTube Is Testing AI Summaries Instead of Video Titles: What It Means for Creators, Discovery, and Search Visibility
item.description: YouTube is testing a feature that, for some users, replaces traditional video titles with AI-generated summaries in the app feed. On the surface, that may look like a small interface experiment. In practice, it touches some of the most important parts of video discovery: click intent, creator con

item.title: [General] Walmart Digital Price Tags Explained: How Electronic Shelf Labels Work, What Changes in Stores, and What Shoppers Should Know

It sounds impressive, but without context it is hard to know what, exactly, that figure tells us. Is it a vanity metric? A sign of genuine product-mark

item.description: Retail marketers have spent the last several years trying to solve the same problem in increasingly crowded digital environments: how to reach shoppers when they are still deciding, still comparing, and still open to influence. That challenge is harder than it looks. Performance marketers want ef

item.title: [General] Meta Adds New In-App Sales Tools for Businesses: What Affiliate Links, Reels Product Tags, One-Tap Checkout and Facebook Shops Expansion Mean
item.description: When a platform makes several commerce changes at once, it usually signals more than a product update. It signals direction. Meta’s latest round of shopping and monetization features does exactly that. Instead of treating Facebook and Instagram as places where product discovery happens and conver

item.title: [General] Do “You Are an Expert” Prompts Hurt AI Accuracy? What the Research Says About Persona Prompting

item.title: [General] Google Adds AI and Bot Labels to Forum and Q&A Structured Data: What digitalSourceType Means for SEO
item.description: Google has updated its structured data documentation for discussion forums and Q&A pages, and the most important change is simple to describe but significant to interpret: publishers can now explicitly label certain forum and Q&A content as AI-generated or bot-generated. At a technical le

item.title: [General] Bing Webmaster Tools Query-Page Mapping: How to Connect Grounding Queries to Cited Pages and Improve AI Visibility

Before this change, site owners could see two useful things, but only in isolation. They could

item.title: [Meta Ads] A Guide to Meta Ads Targeting in 2026

item.description: Meta has made two related moves that matter far beyond a single product update. Second, it is roll
